Entity_Description: qathet Regional District is a regional district in British Columbia, Canada, on the upper Sunshine Coast along the Salish Sea, roughly 118 km northwest of Vancouver. Formerly the Powell River Regional District, it was renamed qathet in 2018 using a Tla'amin word meaning "working together." Its 2021 census population was about 21,000, concentrated in the city of Powell River. The district also takes in Texada, Lasqueti and Savary islands, and its economy has long centred on the historic Powell River pulp-and-paper mill, alongside forestry, fishing and tourism.

Short_Description: When Powell River's giant pulp-and-paper mill opened in 1912, it built one of coastal BC's most storied company towns, and that heritage still defines qathet Regional District, the upper Sunshine Coast district renamed in 2018 from a Tla'amin word meaning "working together." Home to roughly 21,000 people across Powell River, Texada Island and the surrounding coast, this is a ferry-dependent region of heritage mill homes, waterfront cottages and off-grid island properties. {COMPANY_NAME} delivers {SERVICE} built for these conditions, pairing {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP} with genuine knowledge of qathet's marine climate and island logistics. Whether your property sits in the historic Townsite or out on Texada, our {BUSINESS_TYPE} crew shows up ready. Book your {ESTIMATE_TYPE} today. Call {PHONE}.
Long_Description:
Powell River anchors qathet Regional District, and its neighbourhoods carry the region's history in their architecture. The Powell River Townsite, a National Historic District of arts-and-crafts homes built for workers of the once world-leading pulp-and-paper mill, sits alongside Westview, Cranberry and Wildwood, each with its own mix of heritage bungalows, mid-century houses and newer builds. For a {BUSINESS_TYPE}, that variety means no two {SERVICE} jobs look alike, and {COMPANY_NAME} plans each one around the property in front of us.

Climate shapes the work here. The upper Sunshine Coast sees drier summers than Vancouver, but the district is thoroughly marine-exposed along the Salish Sea. Salt air, wind-driven rain off the water and damp winters steadily wear at roofs, siding, decks and exterior finishes, so {SERVICE} in qathet has to be built to hold up to coastal weather rather than inland conditions. We factor that exposure into every recommendation.

Geography adds another layer. qathet is a ferry-dependent region: Texada Island, Lasqueti Island and Savary Island are part of the district, reachable only by boat, and many properties there run on wells, septic systems and off-grid power. Waterfront cottages, seasonal cabins and rural acreages sit next to in-town neighbourhoods, and each carries its own access and logistics challenges. {COMPANY_NAME} schedules around BC Ferries sailings and island realities so your {SERVICE} actually gets done on time.

The local economy, rooted in the historic mill, forestry, fishing, tourism and a growing community of retirees and artists, shapes demand too. Willingdon Beach, the seaside promenade and the working waterfront draw visitors and residents who expect properties kept to a high standard. Whether you own a heritage Townsite home, a Westview rancher or a cabin out on Texada, {COMPANY_NAME} brings {SERVICE_DESCRIPTION} suited to how people actually live and build across qathet.

Choosing a local {BUSINESS_TYPE} matters most where logistics are hardest. We know the difference between a Cranberry lot and a Savary Island beachfront, between a mill-era roofline and a modern coastal build. That is the kind of ground-level knowledge {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP} is built on. For {SERVICE} across qathet Regional District, reach {COMPANY_NAME} at {PHONE}.

- Population: 21,000 (2021 Census)
- Renamed from Powell River Regional District to qathet in 2018, from a Tla'amin word meaning "working together"
- Main community: the city of Powell River, on the upper Sunshine Coast along the Salish Sea
- Includes Texada Island, Lasqueti Island and Savary Island, reachable only by ferry or boat
- The Powell River Townsite is a National Historic District of former mill-worker homes
- Economy rooted in the historic pulp-and-paper mill, forestry, fishing and tourism
- Located about 118 km northwest of Vancouver in the Gulf Islands & Sunshine Coast region
